title: ENHANCING WOMEN'S SAFETY THROUGH AI-POWERED THREAT DETECTION AND MULTI-LAYERED EMERGENCY PROTOCOLS
abstract: In an era of increasing concerns about personal safety, particularly for women, this research proposes a comprehensive, privacy-first ecosystem called 'Shield Her'. The study explores on-device AI for real-time threat detection (screams, stress signals) using TensorFlow Lite, coupled with a multi-mode emergency protocol that ensures stealth activation. We investigate the integration of AES-256 encrypted evidence gathering with potential blockchain-based evidence chains to ensure legal integrity. The research highlights the transition from passive tracking to active, intelligent safety intervention.
journal: International Journal of Creative Research Thoughts (IJCRT)
citation: Singh, R. (2026). Enhancing Women's Safety through AI-Powered Threat Detection and Multi-layered Emergency Protocols. IJCRT, 14(4), 158-172.
